format_discipline.notes: No frontmatter present. First non-empty line is '# Why king minus man plus woman equals queen works' (H1). reading.meta.json exists and parses cleanly. Exactly 6 fixed sections present in correct order: Overview, Key Concepts, Worked Example, In Practice, Key Takeaways, References.

length_budget.notes: meta.json reports 1871 words. Manual spot-check across sections (Overview ~90w, Key Concepts ~700w across 5 subsections, Worked Example ~260w, In Practice ~145w, Key Takeaways ~190w, References ~50w) is consistent with reported count. Falls within [1800, 2200].

citation_discipline.notes: [1] (Edinburgh) cited in Overview, Key Concepts (multiple times), Key Takeaways, and References. [2] (Plotly) cited in Key Concepts, In Practice, and Key Takeaways. sequence_drift.notes: No forward references detected. 'Cosine similarity' (6.9) appears in the corpus Implementation section but is absent from reading.md. All referenced prior concepts (vector, dot product, embedding, distributional hypothesis, Word2Vec, point_in_space, coordinate, axis) are from 6.1-6.7. All new concepts (gender_direction, vector_arithmetic, approximate_equality, nearest_neighbour_answer, embedding_offset) are introduced and defined within this reading.

findings: Minor: 'analogy_direction' is listed as a new concept to introduce but is not explicitly named and bolded as a defined term in the reading. The concept is fully explained through worked examples and narrative, but the label itself is absent. No hard fail — the concept coverage is complete.
beginner_readability.notes: All other new terms (gender_direction, vector_arithmetic, approximate_equality, nearest_neighbour_answer, embedding_offset) are bolded and defined on first use. No paragraph introduces 3 or more new terms simultaneously. Paragraph lengths are consistently 2-4 sentences. No scope creep — content stays within 6.8 boundaries. Only 1 minor readability finding, below the hard-fail threshold of 3.

faithfulness.notes: All claims trace directly to corpus sections 5.1-5.5 and the Hands-On Exercise (§9). The 2D worked example coordinates (man:[1.0,0.5], woman:[1.0,2.5], king:[3.0,0.5], queen:[3.0,2.5]) match corpus §9 exactly. The three reasons for approximation (noisy 300-dim space, 'king' encoding chess contexts, no explicit rule programmed) match corpus §5.4 exactly. Real-world applications (analogy-based search, bias auditing, cross-lingual alignment) match corpus §7 exactly. No new claims introduced beyond corpus.

verdict: PASS — All 6 checks passed. Format is clean, word count 1871 is within [1800,2200], all three sources cited, no forward references, beginner readability is solid with only one minor labelling omission (analogy_direction term not explicitly bolded), and all content is faithful to the corpus.
